feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to block the users from deleting the sales orders in SAP Public cloud?
entry.content:
We would like to restrict the business users from deleting the sales orders in SAP Public cloud. If the subsequent documents like delivery and invoice created, then the system will not allow. 

But if the subsequent document is not created, then it is possible to delete the sales order from the database.

We want to restrict this and advising users to reject the sales orders. Can anyone guide here?

We tried the implementing the BADI "SD_SLS_CHECK_BEFORE_SAVE" but there is no importing parameter which says the deletion is triggered.

entry.title: 我的功能app：定义报表规则无法使用
entry.content: 我的功能app：定义报表规则无法使用。我已经激活了相对应的scope item：1SG 。但是不知道为什么这个功能app无法使用？

entry.title: The business requires a discount field at the Purchase Order item
entry.content:
The business requires a discount field at the Purchase Order item line level in the Create Purchase Order app. Currently, SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud allows discounts to be maintained only through the Conditions tab using condition types. There is no standard discount column available in the PO item overview screen.

We would like to understand whether SAP provides any standard functionality, configuration, personalization, or extensibility option to enable a line-level discount field where users can directly enter a discount amount or percentage without navigating to the Conditions tab.

This requirement is intended to simplify PO creation and improve user efficiency during purchasing transactions.

entry.title: Stock/Requirements list for Multi-materials display.
entry.content:
Dear sap,

How can we display Stock/Requirements list of multi-materials in public cloud edition,  just like MD07 in ECC.

Thanks.

Ying

entry.title: Portugal – Support for Original / Duplicate / Triplicate Identification in Billing Document Output
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

We would like to request clarification regarding the legal document output requirements in Portugal.

According to our customer's business and compliance requirements, billing documents (Invoices, Credit Notes and similar fiscal documents) must be printable in multiple copies identified as:

  • Original
  • Duplicate
  • Triplicate

Additionally, when a document is reprinted, it should be possible to identify the corresponding copy version accordingly.

At the moment, we have not identified a standard functionality in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition that allows the automatic identification of these document copies within the output form.

Could you please clarify:

  • Whether this functionality is currently supported in standard;
  • Whether there are any recommended approaches to achieve this requirement;
  • Whether this requirement is already known by the Localization or Output Management teams.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,

entry.title: Portugal – Support for Original / Duplicate / Triplicate Identification in Billing Document Output
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

We would like to request clarification regarding the legal document output requirements in Portugal.

According to our customer's business and compliance requirements, billing documents (Invoices, Credit Notes and similar fiscal documents) must be printable in multiple copies identified as:

  • Original
  • Duplicate
  • Triplicate

Additionally, when a document is reprinted, it should be possible to identify the corresponding copy version accordingly.

At the moment, we have not identified a standard functionality in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition that allows the automatic identification of these document copies within the output form.

Could you please clarify:

  • Whether this functionality is currently supported in standard;
  • Whether there are any recommended approaches to achieve this requirement;
  • Whether this requirement is already known by the Localization or Output Management teams.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,

entry.title: Integration Scenario SAP Service Cloud V2 and SAP Cloud ERP (aka SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition)
entry.content:
Hello,

would someone now whether there is an available Integration Scenario between SAP Service Cloud V2 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (aka SAP Cloud ERP)? I can only find integration guides to SAP S/4HANA. This seems to be the private cloud edition.
I want to create a work order, service order in public cloud as follow up to an incident in the service cloud.

Thanks for any hints in advance.

Cheers
Georg

entry.title: Unable to Update Sales Order Item Pricing Condition via API_SALES_ORDER_SRV
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I am trying to update the pricing of an existing sales order item in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition via OData API.

Scenario:

  • Sales Order Type: Standard Order
  • The sales order has already been created successfully.
  • Afterwards, I would like to change the price condition of an item using the API.

I am using the following PATCH request against API_SALES_ORDER_SRV:

https://myXXX.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SALES_ORDER_SRV/A_SalesOrderItemPrElement(SalesOrder='278',SalesOrderItem='10',PricingProcedureStep='20',PricingProcedureCounter='1')

Request Body:

{
  "d": {
    "ConditionType": "ZPR0",
    "ConditionRateValue": "0",
    "ConditionCurrency": "EUR"
  }
}

The PATCH request is executed successfully and no error is returned. However, the condition value in the sales order is not updated.

Is it possible to update pricing conditions of an existing sales order via API_SALES_ORDER_SRV in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

Thanks in advance for your help!

Katharina

entry.title: NO_Broker Invoice Balance Due Not Visible After Payment in Supplier/Customer Line Items Report
entry.content:
Dear SAP OEM Support Team,

We are facing an issue in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud related to the visibility of the outstanding invoice balance after payments are posted against supplier/customer invoices.

Issue Details:

  • Supplier and Customer invoices are posted successfully.
  • Payments are subsequently posted against the invoices (including partial payment scenarios).
  • In the Supplier/Customer Line Items report, the invoice documents and payment documents are visible.
  • However, the remaining Invoice Balance Due is not displayed after the payment is applied to the invoice.

Expected Behavior:
After a payment is posted against an invoice, the report should display the updated outstanding balance (remaining amount due) for the invoice, allowing users to easily identify:

  • Original invoice amount
  • Amount paid
  • Remaining balance due/open amount

Actual Behavior:
The report shows the invoice and related payment transactions,

entry.title: Item Text, Note to payee and uploading customer invoices
entry.content:
Hi SAP experts!

My client wants to collect SEPA direct debits from customers. The receivables are to be posted using the ‘Upload customer open items’ app. The payment reference/note to payee should include the document date (FPAYP-BLDAT) and the item text (FPAYP-SGTXT) so that the customer knows what the money is being collected for.

I have learnt that the item text is only transferred to the payment reference if it is entered in the document preceded by an asterisk ‘*’, e.g. *Service Fee. However, if I add an asterisk at the start in the Excel file used to import the receivables, the system removes this asterisk during posting. When entering an outgoing invoice manually, the asterisk remains, but it is removed during automatic import.

How can I resolve this issue?

Thank you and best regards,

Rado

entry.title: Options blocked in app Define Location: Change
entry.content:
Hello everyone.

We've tried to perform a "delete" operation on transaction Define Location: Change, but the option appears to be blocked. We don't know if this is normal or an error. Regarding authorizations, the user has all system roles assigned to avoid authorization issues.

entry.title: 外向交货单80006071无法过账发货;显示被锁定交货，VLA019
entry.content:
使用APP:交货监控器，点击"用于发货"，点击"执行"，选择80006071，点击过账发货，显示报错：0 成功，1 不正确货物移动

消息编号 VLA019
使用APP:消息仪表盘，选择消息"EWM 的出库交货变更 - OB_DLV_CHA/00001 ",打开发现：

在价值链管理中，用户  已在运行流程中锁定交货 80006071   

请问我该如何过账发货交货单80006071？

entry.title: HU Screen Not Visible During Goods Receipt with Production Order
entry.content:
Hi Team,

During Goods Receipt with other document types, I am able to view the HU (Handling Unit) screen under the Warehouse tab.

However, while performing Goods Receipt with a Production Order, the HU screen is not visible in the Warehouse tab. Even i have extended the material to the warehouse storage location still i am not able to see warehouse Tab

 Could you please confirm if any additional configuration is required, or if there are specific settings at the material level that need to be maintained to enable this functionality?

Thank you for your support.

Best regards,
Kiran

entry.title: Maintain another exchange rate type different from type M for the Monitor Purchase Order Items app
entry.content:
I've tried to maintain another exchange rate type to display data for Monitor Purchase Order Items app. I found SAP Note
--- Steps to Reproduce ---

App: Monitor Purchase Order Items


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Availibility contorl is not working in PR
entry.content:
We have cost center budgeting. It is not contorliing budget while creating PR but contorlling while PO.

Is this bug? in public cloud

entry.title: Purchasing Document and Purchasing Document Item Not Displayed in Supplier Line Items App
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We have observed an issue in the Supplier Line Items app. The columns Purchasing Document and Purchasing Document Item are available in the report layout; however, the values are not being displayed. The Purchasing Document Item column is showing "00000", and the Purchasing Document column is blank for the relevant entries.

Could you please confirm:

  • Whether this is the standard system behavior.
  • If any configuration or settings are required to display these values.

Please find the attached screenshot for reference.

Thank you for your support.

Best Regards,
Ankam Chandu.

entry.title: Issue with Automatic Clearing by Ignoring Partial Receipts during Reprocess Bank statements
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

We are facing an issue related to automatic clearing in our system and would appreciate your guidance.

Scenario:

We have multiple invoices for a customer, and partial Receipts have already been received and applied to some of these invoices. In certain cases, when a new incoming Receipt is posted, the system automatically clears the invoice because the assignment/reference number matches the invoice and the payment amount exactly matches the invoice amount and ignores the Partial receipts already available.

  • The system clears the invoice with the latest receipt.
  • Previously applied partial receipts remain as open items.
  • This creates confusion in tracking open items and leads to inconsistencies in account balances.
  • The issue becomes more critical in cases where excess Receipts need to be identified and handled correctly, especially for refund processing.

Ideally, in scenarios where partial payments are already applied to an invoice, automatic clearing with a new receipt should not occur without proper validation and should not be automated.

 Thanks

Naveen Kumar

entry.title: Can the reconciliation / payable account be determined by transaction origin (SD vs FI) for a single
entry.content:
**Title:**
Can the reconciliation / payable account be determined by transaction origin (SD vs FI) for a single Business Partner in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

**Body:**

We are migrating from SAP ECC to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

In our current ECC landscape, the same external counterparty is maintained as **two separate master records** — one used through the SD process flow and one posted to directly from FI. In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition we plan to consolidate them into a **single Business Partner**.

(For clarity: this Business Partner is set up as a *customer-supplier* — it is both invoiced through SD-driven flows and posted to directly in FI, and we owe a liability to it.)

Our accounting requirement is that the **payable / reconciliation account differs depending on where the posting originates**:

- Liability generated from an **SD-driven** process → **trade accounts payable**
- Liability posted **directly in FI** → **other (non-trade) payable**

We are already aware of the following standard constraints in Public Edition:
- *Alternative reconciliation accounts* (SSCUI 102631) only take effect for manual FI invoice apps where the reconciliation account field is set ready-for-input; they are not applied to automatic SD-driven postings.
- *Special G/L indicators* are pre-defined by SAP and cannot be newly created in Public Edition.

**Questions**
1. For one and the same Business Partner, is it possible to have the reconciliation account determined automatically based on the originating module (SD vs FI)?
2. Given the constraints above, is there any standard approach in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to achieve origin-based determination at all?
3. If standard cannot cover it, does it require custom development? Under Public Cloud extensibility (BAdI / key-user / developer extensibility), which option — if any — would apply, or is splitting into two Business Partners the only viable path?

System: S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (latest active release)
Tags: `S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition`, `FI`, `Business Partner`, `Reconciliation Account`

entry.title: ERS-generated payables from a Purchase Order — can the reconciliation account be influenced (alterna
entry.content:
**Title:**
ERS-generated payables from a Purchase Order — can the reconciliation account be influenced (alternative / non-trade payable) for the same supplier in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

**Body:**

In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, when we settle a Purchase Order via **ERS (Evaluated Receipt Settlement)**, the supplier invoice is posted **automatically** from the PO and goods-receipt data, with no manual line-item interaction.

Because there is no manual posting step, there appears to be **no opportunity to choose an alternative reconciliation account** — unlike the manual Supplier Invoice apps (e.g. FB60 / F0859), where the reconciliation account can be made ready-for-input and changed at posting time. As a result, the ERS-generated payable always posts to the reconciliation account taken from the supplier (Business Partner) master.

However, for the **same supplier**, we have business cases requiring **both** postings:
- some liabilities to **trade payable**
- others to **other / non-trade payable**

**Questions**
1. When a payable is created automatically via ERS, is there any standard way to influence or override the reconciliation account, or is the master-data reconciliation account always enforced?
2. For a single supplier needing both trade payable and non-trade payable, what is the recommended standard approach in Public Edition? Is creating a second supplier Business Partner (running ERS only on the trade-payable one) effectively the only viable option?
3. Are there any alternatives we are missing under Public Cloud constraints (e.g. special G/L handling via Automatic Account Determination, or handling the non-trade portion through a separate manual process)?

System: S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (latest active release)
Tags: `S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition`, `MM`, `ERS`, `Reconciliation Account`, `FI-MM Integration`

entry.title: Pick replenishment not permitted; storage type (group) not maintained
entry.content:
Hi,

We have set up planned replenishment for two of our storage types in SAP using the customizing activity ‘Activate Replenishment Strategies in Storage type’. However, when we now create replenishment warehouse tasks, the tasks are created to be taken from the same storage type/bin as were they need to be placed. The issue in this lies in the stock removal control indicator, which checks for the storage type where the products are before the stock in our main warehouse. I therefore want to change the replenishment strategy to take stock from the main storage type only. However, when I add this, an error arises: Pick replenishment not permitted; storage type (group) not maintained. In my view, the group and storage type are maintained correctly but i need your expertise in this.

Could you help me resolve this issue?
